tasks.title: Fix Ctrl+B Keyboard Shortcut Conflict
tasks.description: Resolve conflict between Ctrl+B (Bold formatting) and File Tree toggle by changing File Tree to Ctrl+Shift+E and auditing all shortcuts.
tasks.details: In `src/config/settings.rs` lines 606/625: 1) Retain Ctrl+B for bold; 2) Change file tree toggle to Ctrl+Shift+E (VS Code standard); 3) Audit all shortcuts using keybinding conflict detection logic; 4) Update settings serialization/deserialization. Use Rust's `keymap` crate best practices for conflict-free bindings.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Select text → Ctrl+B → text boldens, file tree unchanged; 2. Ctrl+Shift+E → file tree toggles; 3. Test all shortcuts for conflicts; 4. Verify settings persist across restarts.

tasks.title: Fix Undo After Text Formatting
tasks.description: Ensure formatting operations (bold, italic, etc.) create discrete undo entries instead of deleting text.
tasks.details: Modify undo stack implementation: 1) Capture pre-format text state; 2) Record format as atomic operation with exact char boundaries; 3) On Ctrl+Z, restore to pre-format state preserving text content; 4) Handle all formats (bold/italic/strikethrough). Implement using transactional edit model with `rope` data structure for efficient range operations.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Type 'hello world'; 2. Select 'world' → Ctrl+B; 3. Ctrl+Z → 'hello world' (bold removed); 4. Test Ctrl+Y redo; 5. Verify all formatting types; 6. Test chained formats.

tasks.title: Fix Multiline Blockquote Rendering
tasks.description: Group consecutive blockquote lines (separated by soft breaks) into single continuous block.
tasks.details: In `src/markdown/` parser: 1) Modify blockquote state machine to track consecutive '>' lines; 2) Treat two trailing spaces as soft break, not blockquote terminator; 3) Merge into single blockquote node; 4) Preserve internal line breaks. Update pulldown-cmark or custom parser with `blockquote_continuation` logic.
tasks.testStrategy: Input: '> Line1  \n> Line2' → Single blockquote with line break inside; Test edge cases: mixed content, nested blockquotes, varying whitespace.

tasks.title: Preserve Selection After Formatting
tasks.description: Maintain text selection after applying formats, adjusting for added syntax characters (** etc.).
tasks.details: In formatting handler: 1) Store original selection {start, end}; 2) Apply format (e.g., insert '**'); 3) Calculate new boundaries accounting for prefix/suffix chars; 4) Restore selection to formatted range. Implement with `SelectionRange::adjust_for_insertions()` method.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Select 'hello' → Ctrl+B → '**hello**' selected; 2. Ctrl+I → '***hello***' selected; 3. Test all formats; 4. Verify cursor position preserved.

tasks.title: Integrate PR #74 Terminal & Productivity Hub
tasks.description: Merge PR #74 with basic functionality: terminal toggle (Ctrl+`), productivity hub (Ctrl+Shift+H).
tasks.details: 1) Cherry-pick/merge PR #74 commits; 2) Wire terminal toggle to Ctrl+` (existing toolbar); 3) Wire productivity hub to Ctrl+Shift+H; 4) Remove 'Coming Soon' placeholders; 5) Apply Ferrite design language CSS; 6) Test dark/light theme compatibility.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Ctrl+` → terminal toggles; 2. Ctrl+Shift+H → productivity hub; 3. Verify UI matches design system; 4. Test theme switching; 5. No console errors.

tasks.title: Implement CJK First-Line Indentation
tasks.description: Add Chinese (2em) / Japanese (1em) first-line paragraph indentation based on language setting.
tasks.details: 1) Add CJK setting to `src/config/settings.rs`; 2) Detect language via Unicode ranges or user setting; 3) Inject `text-indent: 2em/1em` CSS for CJK paragraphs; 4) Target only first line using `p::first-line` or JS polyfill; 5) Use full-width em units.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Enable CJK (Chinese) → Chinese paragraphs show 2em first-line indent; 2. Japanese → 1em; 3. English → no indent; 4. Verify only first line affected.

tasks.title: Terminal Memory Optimization (10k Line Cap)
tasks.description: Reduce baseline memory from 300MB to ≤150MB by implementing circular scrollback buffer.
tasks.details: 1) Profile with `heaptrack`/`valgrind`; 2) Replace linear terminal buffer with circular buffer (VecDeque, capacity 10,000 lines); 3) Implement line wrapping/eviction logic; 4) Ensure PTY handles drop on close (`drop(pty)` in destructor); 5) Test with 1,000+ tasks.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Baseline memory ≤150MB; 2. Terminal scrollback caps at 10k lines; 3. Oldest lines evict correctly; 4. No memory leaks (valgrind); 5. Performance with large task lists.

tasks.title: Implement Terminal Error Handling
tasks.description: Add user-friendly PTY error notifications and workspace sync failure logging.
tasks.details: 1) Catch PTY spawn failures → toast notification ('Terminal failed to start'); 2) Workspace sync errors → structured logging (`log::error!`); 3) Add recovery hints ('Check shell path', 'Restart app'); 4) Graceful terminal close handling.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Invalid shell path → user-friendly error toast; 2. Network failure → sync logged, no crash; 3. Terminal close → clean resource cleanup; 4. Error recovery works.

tasks.title: Configure Windows Code Signing
tasks.description: Set up SignPath.io signing for ferrite.exe and MSI installer.
tasks.details: 1) Complete SignPath.io OSS approval; 2) Configure `.signpath/artifact-configuration.xml`; 3) Add signing step to Windows CI pipeline (GitHub Actions); 4) Sign ferrite.exe and ferrite-x.x.x-x64.msi; 5) Verify signatures with `sigcheck`.
tasks.testStrategy: 1. Windows Defender scan → no false positives; 2. `sigcheck -i ferrite.exe` shows valid signature; 3. MSI installer signed; 4. CI pipeline produces signed artifacts.

subtasks.title: Update GitHub Actions workflow to use release-signing policy
subtasks.description: Switch the signing-policy-slug from 'test-signing' to 'release-signing' in the .github/workflows/release.yml file.
subtasks.details: Edit .github/workflows/release.yml to change the SignPath policy slug parameter from 'test-signing' to 'release-signing' in the signing step. Commit and push the change to trigger a test release build.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify workflow runs successfully and uses production certificate by checking signing logs.

subtasks.title: Add SignPath sponsor attribution to README.md
subtasks.description: Insert required SignPath.io attribution text into the README.md file per OSS terms.
subtasks.details: Add the exact text: 'Free code signing on Windows provided by SignPath.io, certificate by SignPath Foundation' to an appropriate section in README.md, such as a 'Code Signing' or 'Acknowledgments' section.
subtasks.testStrategy: Confirm text appears correctly in rendered README on GitHub repository page.

subtasks.title: Document GitHub repository secrets requirements
subtasks.description: Verify and document SIGNPATH_API_TOKEN and SIGNPATH_ORGANIZATION_ID secrets in repository documentation.
subtasks.details: Add a section to README.md or CONTRIBUTING.md explaining required GitHub repository secrets: SIGNPATH_API_TOKEN and SIGNPATH_ORGANIZATION_ID, with instructions on obtaining them from SignPath dashboard.
subtasks.testStrategy: Check that secrets documentation is clear and complete in README.

subtasks.title: Update antivirus false-positive section in README
subtasks.description: Revise README section on antivirus false positives to reflect code signing benefits.
subtasks.details: Update the antivirus false-positive section in README.md to note that code signing with production certificate should significantly reduce false positives on Windows Defender and other AV scanners.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify updated text accurately reflects signing status and benefits.

subtasks.title: Verify and update GitHub workflow permissions for SignPath
subtasks.description: Ensure release workflow has correct permissions for SignPath trusted build verification.
subtasks.details: In .github/workflows/release.yml, confirm or add 'permissions: { id-token: write }' at workflow level. Also verify any other required permissions for OIDC authentication with SignPath.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run workflow and confirm no permission errors; check signed artifacts have trusted build metadata.

tasks.title: Comprehensive Regression Testing & Release Prep
tasks.description: Verify all fixes, test cross-platform, update release notes, prepare v0.2.6.1 release.
tasks.details: 1) Run full test suite; 2) Manual testing checklist (P0-P4); 3) Cross-platform verification (Windows priority); 4) Update CHANGELOG.md with release notes; 5) Tag v0.2.6.1; 6) Create GitHub release.
tasks.testStrategy: Complete testing checklist: All P0 bugs fixed, memory ≤150MB, terminal works, CJK correct, no regressions, Windows signed builds clean.
